policy: Expanded snippets do not promote candidates. They only strengthen display and audit of rows already in authoritative_scope_rows.

records.snippet_en: 4.2 Environmental information GHG emissions related to energy (Scopes 1 and 2), car fleet and business travel decreased by 38% between 2019 and 2024, and amounted to 101,033 tons equivalent CO2 (tCO2 eq) in 2024. Those emissions remained flat as compared to 2023, as the resumption of business travel has been offset by a decrease in vehicle fleet and energy emissions. The target of decrease by 20% between 2025 and 2019 on the GHG emissions related to energy, car fleet, business travel and IT was already reached in 2024 with a reduction of 30%. Analysis by Scope GHG emissions related to Scope 1 was 21,054 tCO2 eq (1) in 2024, decreasing by 4% as compared to 2023 mainly due to the switch to hybrid and electrical vehicles in several countries this year. In 2024, 22% of the km travelled by AXA's Group's vehicle fleet were covered by hybrid and electric cars versus 11% last year. The decrease in GHG emissions from vehicle fleet reach 26% versus 2019 this year. GHG emissions from Scope 2 Market‑based was 23,146 tCO2 eq (2) in 2024, decreasing by 11% as compared to 2023 driven by a lower energy consumption and an increase in the share of renewable energy. GHG emissions from Scope 3.6‑Business travelling increased by 7%. Despite the measures taken at entity level to reduce their impacts, several inter‑continent events occurred in 2024, leading to an increase in the number of km travelled this year. Yet, the emissio

records.snippet_en: cle fleet and energy emissions. The target of decrease by 20% between 2025 and 2019 on the GHG emissions related to energy, car fleet, business travel and IT was already reached in 2024 with a reduction of 30%. Analysis by Scope GHG emissions related to Scope 1 was 21,054 tCO2 eq (1) in 2024, decreasing by 4% as compared to 2023 mainly due to the switch to hybrid and electrical vehicles in several countries this year. In 2024, 22% of the km travelled by AXA's Group's vehicle fleet were covered by hybrid and electric cars versus 11% last year. The decrease in GHG emissions from vehicle fleet reach 26% versus 2019 this year. GHG emissions from Scope 2 Market‑based was 23,146 tCO2 eq (2) in 2024, decreasing by 11% as compared to 2023 driven by a lower energy consumption and an increase in the share of renewable energy. GHG emissions from Scope 3.6‑Business travelling increased by 7%. Despite the measures taken at entity level to reduce their impacts, several inter‑continent events occurred in 2024, leading to an increase in the number of km travelled this year. Yet, the emissions associated to business travel decreased by 29% compared to 2019, on track regarding the 2030 target of 40% reduction and well beyond the 2025 target of 18% reduction, confirming sustained changes of habit. GHG emission from Scope 3.1‑Purchased goods and services remained stable, however the portion related to cloud computing and data centers services increased by 23% due to the transfer of services from

GHG emissions from Scope 3.6‑Business travelling increased by 7%. Despite the measures taken at entity level to reduce their impacts, several inter‑continent events occurred in 2024, leading to an increase in the number of km travelled this year. Yet, the emissions associated to business travel decreased by 29% compared to 2019, on track regarding the 2030 target of 40% reduction and well beyond the 2025 target of 18% reduction, confirming sustained changes of habit. r126axa__scope2_____2023__175__0023146__.snippet_en: cle fleet and energy emissions. The target of decrease by 20% between 2025 and 2019 on the GHG emissions related to energy, car fleet, business travel and IT was already reached in 2024 with a reduction of 30%. Analysis by Scope GHG emissions related to Scope 1 was 21,054 tCO2 eq (1) in 2024, decreasing by 4% as compared to 2023 mainly due to the switch to hybrid and electrical vehicles in several countries this year. In 2024, 22% of the km travelled by AXA's Group's vehicle fleet were covered by hybrid and electric cars versus 11% last year. The decrease in GHG emissions from vehicle fleet reach 26% versus 2019 this year. GHG emissions from Scope 2 Market‑based was 23,146 tCO2 eq (2) in 2024, decreasing by 11% as compared to 2023 driven by a lower energy consumption and an increase in the share of renewable energy. GHG emissions from Scope 3.6‑Business travelling increased by 7%. Despite the measures taken at entity level to reduce their impacts, several inter‑continent events occurred in 2024, leading to an increase in the number of km travelled this year. Yet, the emissions associated to business travel decreased by 29% compared to 2019, on track regarding the 2030 target of 40% reduction and well beyond the 2025 target of 18% reduction, confirming sustained changes of habit.
